# PAV vs STL-$\\rho$ (auto-generated; superseded)

> **Superseded by `pav_v2.md` (2026-04-26).** The math-rigor audit on 2026-04-26 flagged this comparison as weak in three specific ways: no model selection (fixed 30 epochs, no hidden-width sweep), offline kNN labels rather than Setlur §3.2 on-policy rollouts, and a degenerate repressilator corpus presented as an "anti-informative" finding. The strengthened comparison lives at `paper/pav_v2.md`. This file is kept as a historical record only --- do not cite it as the canonical PAV vs STL number.

Regenerated by `scripts/run_pav_comparison.py`; do not hand-edit. PAV (Process Advantage Verifier; Setlur et al. 2024, [arXiv:2410.08146](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.08146)) is the learned process-reward baseline. We compare its held-out discriminability against the closed-form STL-$\\rho$ verifier on the canonical trajectory store.

| task                  | spec                         | n_train | n_test | STL AUC | PAV AUC | PAV − STL | crossover_n | PAV train (s) | STL score (s) |
| --------------------- | ---------------------------- | ------: | -----: | ------: | ------: | --------: | ----------: | ------------: | ------------: |
| bio_ode.repressilator | `bio_ode.repressilator.easy` |    2000 |    400 |   1.000 |   0.000 |    -1.000 |           - |          1.36 |        0.0515 |
| glucose_insulin       | `glucose_insulin.tir.easy`   |    2000 |    400 |   1.000 |   0.819 |    -0.181 |           - |          1.45 |        0.0507 |

Spearman rank correlation:

| task                  | PAV vs success | STL vs success | PAV vs $\\rho$ | STL vs $\\rho$ |
| --------------------- | -------------: | -------------: | -------------: | -------------: |
| bio_ode.repressilator |         -0.867 |         +0.961 |         -0.828 |         +1.000 |
| glucose_insulin       |         +0.557 |         +0.874 |         +0.773 |         +1.000 |

Sample efficiency (each row a PAV refit at the indicated train-set size; STL AUC is constant in train size):

bio_ode.repressilator (`bio_ode.repressilator.easy`):

| n_train | PAV AUC | PAV train (s) | PAV final train MSE | PAV final val MSE |
| ------: | ------: | ------------: | ------------------: | ----------------: |
|     100 |   0.056 |          0.31 |             0.03753 |           0.02217 |
|     500 |   0.064 |          0.52 |             0.03579 |           0.06225 |
|    1000 |   0.000 |          0.78 |             0.03452 |           0.06381 |
|    2000 |   0.000 |          0.83 |             0.03061 |           0.04432 |

![sample efficiency: bio_ode.repressilator](/Users/abdullahalghamdi/stl-seed/runs/pav_comparison/bio_ode.repressilator__sample_efficiency.png)

glucose_insulin (`glucose_insulin.tir.easy`):

| n_train | PAV AUC | PAV train (s) | PAV final train MSE | PAV final val MSE |
| ------: | ------: | ------------: | ------------------: | ----------------: |
|     100 |   0.912 |          0.34 |             0.02703 |           0.00779 |
|     500 |   0.026 |          0.57 |             0.02924 |           0.02975 |
|    1000 |   0.077 |          0.83 |             0.03099 |           0.02465 |
|    2000 |   0.774 |          1.12 |             0.02942 |           0.02633 |

![sample efficiency: glucose_insulin](/Users/abdullahalghamdi/stl-seed/runs/pav_comparison/glucose_insulin__sample_efficiency.png)

STL-$\\rho$ needs zero training and a single closed-form pass per trajectory (`stl_seed/stl/evaluator.py`). PAV needs the full training pool, the per-step MC label computation (kNN), and an MLP fit. The PAV train column above is the wall-clock for the canonical PAV fit at the largest train size.

PAV AUC = 0.000 on bio_ode.repressilator reflects a 0% terminal-success rate in the random pool, which collapses the kNN MC estimator. The glucose_insulin contrast (1.000 vs 0.819) is the cleaner comparison.

Provenance: PAV `src/stl_seed/baselines/pav.py`; harness `src/stl_seed/baselines/comparison.py`; script `scripts/run_pav_comparison.py`; canonical trajectories `data/canonical/`.
